cards.front: What is a file system structure?
cards.back: A hierarchical organization of files and directories on a storage device
The topmost directory in a file system hierarchy, containing all other directories and files
A folder that contains other directories and files, used to organize files and provide access to them
A collection of data stored on a storage device, such as a text file or an image file

cards.front: What is a file system hierarchy standard (FHS)
cards.back: A standard for organizing file systems in Unix-like operating systems, specifying the structure and naming conventions for directories and files
